title: Lucretia/angular js reporting wizard
author: EmanuelJauregui

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR migrates the legacy Reporting Wizard launch path away from the old ASPX “wndReportingWizard” window-manager flow to a new Angular modal-based wizard (with a legacy JS bridge), while also consolidating report list generation on the server and adding supporting Angular UI/test infrastructure.

**Changes:**
- Replace legacy ReportingWizard window usage with an Angular modal dialog + `AngularReportingWizard` bridge (shell + fdef iframe support).
- Introduce new report-wizard dialog models/services/components (grid, project selector, email selector) plus new API endpoints for wizard init + report initiation.
- Refactor server-side report retrieval into option-driven APIs (`ReportsForTableTypeOptions` / `ReportsListOptions`) and update admin/ASP.NET consumers.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 117 out of 120 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

**Core/Web Layer/WebUI/ps-ng/src/app/Reporting/dialog/services/ReportWizardGridService.ts:60**
* `initializeGrid` accepts `null | undefined`, but inside the `shouldShowGrid` branch it passes `data` directly to `sortAttachments(attachments: AttachmentRow[])`. This relies on a runtime invariant instead of a type guard, and it also leaves `_attachments` unchanged when `data` is null/undefined (risking stale attachments if `initializeGrid` is called again).
</details>

review_comments.body: Use `[style.width]` binding instead of `[ngStyle]` (project convention: prefer native `[style]` bindings over `ngStyle`).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Use `[class.*]` binding instead of `[ngClass]` for a single conditional class (project convention: prefer native `[class]` bindings over `ngClass`).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Use modern Angular control flow (`@if`) instead of `*ngIf` for conditional rendering in templates (keeps consistency with the rest of the template style).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `ResetAdHocReportSettingsForListView` was changed to return `Promise<any>` and dropped the typed `Get<AdHocReportSettings>(...)` call. This loses type safety for callers like `main-container.component.ts` that treat the response as `AdHocReportSettings`.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: CSS guideline: avoid using `--t-font-size-*` / `--t-font-weight-*` design-token variables for typography in component CSS (use literal px/rem values instead).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `ResetAdHocReportSettingsForListView` was changed to return `Promise<any>` and calls `Get` without a type argument, which drops type safety (the controller returns `AdHocReportSettings`). Keeping this typed prevents accidental breaking changes and improves IDE support.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `GetReportWizardInitData` returns `Promise<ReportWizardInitData>` but calls `this.Get(request)` without the generic type, so the payload is effectively `any` and the return type is unchecked.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `InitiateReportRequest` returns `Promise<number>` but calls `Post(...)` without a type argument, so the compiler can't verify the API actually returns a number.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: These DMS `UsersAndGroupsPopup.*` files were added to the generated `Packer.manifest`, but the PR is focused on the Reporting Wizard migration. If these entries are unrelated to this change, they should be removed to avoid unintentionally expanding the packaged surface area (or moved/justified in the generator `Packer.targets`).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: When AddBlankPlaceholder is enabled, inserting the default report at index 0 pushes the blank placeholder down to index 1. Callers like NotificationSettings that request a blank placeholder expect it to remain the first item.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: ResetAdHocReportSettingsForListView was widened to Promise<any>, which drops the existing AdHocReportSettings typing and weakens API consumers (e.g., adhoc-report-settings.component.ts).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: CSS guideline: avoid using design-token CSS variables (e.g. --t-font-size-*, --t-font-weight-*) for typography in component CSS; use literal px/rem values instead.
review_comments.author: Copilot

+using GCCM.Common.SystemFramework;
+
+namespace ProjectSight.Common.Components
+{
+    /// <summary>
+    /// Options for loading reports for a table type. Used by <c>ReportSystem.GetReportsForTableType</c> and
+    /// <c>ReportSystem.GetReportsForTableTypeList</c>, which forward these values to <c>GetReportsForTableTypeInternal</c>.
+    /// </summary>
+    public struct ReportsForTableTypeOptions
+    {
+        /// <summary>
+        /// Primary table type to load reports for. <see cref="PrologTable.Types.None"/> retrieves all reports when permitted by the caller path.
+        /// </summary>
+        public PrologTable.Types TableType { get; set; }
+
+        /// <summary>
+        /// When true, denied-report security is not applied (e.g. Web Admin).
+        /// </summary>
+        public bool DoNotLimitDeniedReports { get; set; }
+
+        /// <summary>
+        /// When true, stops after the first matching report (existence checks).
+        /// </summary>
+        public bool ReturnOnFirstFound { get; set; }
+
+        /// <summary>
+        /// When true, includes alternate table types (Budget-related expansion).
+        /// </summary>
+        public bool OkToIncludeAlternateTableTypes { get; set; }
+    }
+}

+using GCCM.Common.SystemFramework;
+
+namespace ProjectSight.Common.Components
+{
+    /// <summary>
+    /// Options for loading reports for a table type. Used by <c>ReportSystem.GetReportsForTableType</c> and
+    /// <c>ReportSystem.GetReportsForTableTypeList</c>, which forward these values to <c>GetReportsForTableTypeInternal</c>.
+    /// </summary>
+    public struct ReportsListOptions
+    {
+        /// <summary>
+        /// The actual table type for this report.
+        /// This is used in very specific cases where the table type for a report is different than the table type that it is maintained in.
+        /// The only current example is BudgetSnapshotItems reports which are maintained in the Budget settings because the two tables have an identical structure.
+        /// </summary>
+        public PrologTable.Types? ActualTableType { get; set; }
+
+        /// <summary>
+        /// When true, the report is for multiple records.
+        /// </summary>
+        public bool? IsForMultipleRecords { get; set; }
+
+        /// <summary>
+        /// When true, adds a blank item at the beginning of the list to serve as a placeholder for "no report selected". This is used when loading reports for a dropdown that allows an optional report selection
+        /// </summary>
+        public bool AddBlankPlaceholder { get; set; }
+
+        /// <summary>
+        /// When true, the not enabled reports will not be included in the list. This is used when loading reports for a dropdown that should not show reports that are not enabled, such as the report selection for the links panel.
+        /// </summary>
+        public bool IncludeOnlyEnabledReports { get; set; }
+
+    }
+}

+import { inject, Injectable, signal } from "@angular/core";
+
+import { take } from "rxjs";
+
+import { ModalContent, ModalHostService } from "shared/components/modal-host/modal-host.service";
+
+import { ApplicationContextService } from "shared/services/ApplicationContextService";
+
+import { CurrentContextService } from "shared/services/CurrentContextService";
+
+import { ReportWizardDialogComponent } from "../components/report-wizard-dialog/report-wizard-dialog.component";
+
+import { ReportingOptions } from "../models/ReportingOptions";
+
+import { ReportWizardLoadArgs } from "../models/ReportWizardLoadArgs";
+
+import { ReportWizardLoadRequest } from "../models/report-wizard-load-request";
+
+import { PrologTableTypes } from "shared/enums/PrologTableTypes";
+
+import { wemiExceptionService } from "shared/support/wemiExceptionService";
+
+import { SelectContactPopoverService } from "components/select-contact-popover/select-contact-popover.service";
+
+import { ContactDetailsPopupService } from "shared/controls/contact-details/ContactDetailsPopupService";
+
+
+
+export { buildReportWizardOpenParams, ReportWizardOpenParams } from "../models/report-wizard-open-params";
+
+
+
+interface OpenRequest {
+
+    keyValues: string;
+
+    options: ReportingOptions;
+
+    tableType: PrologTableTypes;
+
+    isForMultipleRecords: boolean;
+
+    actualTableType: number;
+
+    showProjectSelection: boolean;
+
+    callerProjectId?: number;
+
+    resolve: (value: boolean | undefined) => void;
+
+}
+
+
+
+/**
+ * Root-scoped modal entry: opens/closes the report wizard via {@link ModalHostService}.
+ * Serializes overlapping opens: a new {@link open} supersedes one still in flight.
+ *
+ * Dialog-scoped work (grid, submit pipeline, panel events) lives on the dialog component
+ * providers, not here.
+ */
+
+@Injectable({ providedIn: "root" })
+
+export class ReportWizardDialogService {
+
+    /** Optional mirror for unit tests; load is driven by {@link ModalHostService#requestLoad}. */
+
+    public readonly loadArgs = signal<ReportWizardLoadArgs | null>(null);
+
+
+
+    private _wizardContent: ModalContent | null = null;
+
+
+
+    private _openGeneration = 0;
+
+
+
+    private readonly _wemiException = inject(wemiExceptionService);
+
+
+
+    private readonly _applicationContext = inject(ApplicationContextService);
+
+
+
+    private readonly _modalHost = inject(ModalHostService);
+
+
+
+    /**
+
+     * Opens the reporting wizard in modal-host.
+
+     * Uses {@link ApplicationContextService#injector} from the active application context.
+
+     */
+
+    public open(
+
+        keyValues: string,
+
+        options: ReportingOptions,
+
+        tableType: PrologTableTypes,
+
+        isForMultipleRecords: boolean,
+
+        actualTableType: number,
+
+        showProjectSelection: boolean = false,
+
+        callerProjectId?: number
+
+    ): Promise<boolean | undefined> {
+
+        if (this._modalHost.activeContent()?.settings.targetComponentType === ReportWizardDialogComponent.COMPONENT_TYPE) {
+
+            this.close(undefined);
+
+        }
+
+
+
+        const generation = ++this._openGeneration;
+
+
+
+        return new Promise<boolean | undefined>((resolve) => {
+
+            this.runOpen(
+
+                {
+
+                    keyValues,
+
+                    options,
+
+                    tableType,
+
+                    isForMultipleRecords,
+
+                    actualTableType,
+
+                    showProjectSelection,
+
+                    callerProjectId,
+
+                    resolve,
+
+                },
+
+                generation
+
+            );
+
+        });
+
+    }
+
+
+
+    public close(result?: boolean): void {
+
+        this.loadArgs.set(null);
+
+        this._modalHost.close(result);
+
+    }
+
+
+
+    private runOpen(req: OpenRequest, generation: number): void {
+
+        const hostInjector = this._applicationContext.injector;
+
+        if (!hostInjector) {
+
+            this._wemiException.handlePromiseCatch(new Error("Report wizard: no active application context injector"));
+
+            req.resolve(undefined);
+
+            return;
+
+        }
+
+
+
+        let context: CurrentContextService;
+
+        try {
+
+            context = hostInjector.get(CurrentContextService);
+
+        } catch (reason: unknown) {
+
+            this._wemiException.handlePromiseCatch(reason);
+
+            req.resolve(undefined);
+
+            return;
+
+        }
+
+
+
+        const loadArgs = new ReportWizardLoadArgs(
+
+            req.keyValues,
+
+            req.options,
+
+            req.tableType,
+
+            req.isForMultipleRecords,
+
+            req.actualTableType,
+
+            req.showProjectSelection,
+
+            req.callerProjectId
+
+        );
+
+
+
+        this.loadArgs.set(loadArgs);
+
+
+
+        const settings = ReportWizardDialogComponent.getModalSettings();
+
+        // DEF iframe pages (fdef bootstrap) provide SelectContactPopoverService at the app root
+        // via PageBaseEditorProviders, and the ASPX page already renders its own <select-contact-popover>.
+        // In that case we reuse the host's service so the page's single popover handles display.
+        // Shell pages (Angular routes) don't have it at the route-resolver level, so the wizard
+        // provides its own instance and renders its own <select-contact-popover> inside the modal.
+        // TODO: Remove this conditional once ASPX DEF pages are fully migrated (no iframes).
+        const hasHostPopover = !!hostInjector.get(SelectContactPopoverService, null);
+
+        const additionalProviders = [
+            ...(hasHostPopover ? [] : [SelectContactPopoverService, ContactDetailsPopupService]),
+        ];
+
+
+
+        this._wizardContent = new ModalContent(ReportWizardDialogComponent, settings, context, additionalProviders);
+
+
+
+        settings.closedEvent.pipe(take(1)).subscribe((result) => {
+
+            if (generation !== this._openGeneration) {
+
+                // Superseded by a newer open(): resolve as cancelled so the original
+
+                // caller never hangs, and leave `_wizardContent` alone (it belongs to
+
+                // whichever open is now current).
+
+                req.resolve(undefined);
+
+                return;
+
+            }
+
+            this._wizardContent = null;
+
+            req.resolve(result);
+
+        });
+
+
+
+        this._modalHost.show(this._wizardContent);
+
+        this._modalHost.requestLoad(new ReportWizardLoadRequest(loadArgs));
+
+    }
+
+}
+

+import { PrologTableTypes } from "shared/enums/PrologTableTypes";
+
+/**
+ * Table-type rules for the report wizard (client-side chrome and portfolio-scope helpers).
+ * Chrome matches legacy ReportingWizard / former server `ReportWizardInitData` behavior.
+ * `GetReportWizardInitData` returns defaults and preloaded rows only; preload skipping for some tables
+ * remains server-side in `ReportWizardInitDataRules.SkipsSingleRecordReportRequestPreload` (C#).
+ *
+ * Table types that are ALWAYS portfolio-level (never project-scoped).
+ * Used to suppress project selection and force portfolio-scope API calls.
+ * Note: Company and POCatalog have [DatabaseSpecific] in C# but appear in
+ * both Portfolio Settings and Project Settings, so they are NOT listed here —
+ * their project scope depends on the navigation context.
+ */
+const DATABASE_SPECIFIC_TYPES = new Set<number>([
+    PrologTableTypes.Contact,
+    PrologTableTypes.User,
+    PrologTableTypes.UserGroup,
+    PrologTableTypes.Organization,
+    PrologTableTypes.ApplicationUser,
+    PrologTableTypes.Account,
+    PrologTableTypes.SystemDetails,
+]);
+
+export function typeIsDatabaseSpecific(tableType: number): boolean {
+    return DATABASE_SPECIFIC_TYPES.has(tableType);
+}
+
+/**
+ * Matches C# PrologTable.TypeIsDatabaseSpecific for wizard chrome purposes.
+ * Company and POCatalog are omitted from typeIsDatabaseSpecific() so portfolio-scope API calls
+ * can still follow callerProjectId; they are added here because the
+ * project-selection panel is still hidden for them in the wizard.
+ * Consumed internally by shouldShowProjectSelection; exported only for the characterization
+ * tests that lock down the table-type coverage.
+ */
+export function typeMatchesReportWizardDatabaseSpecific(tableType: number): boolean {
+    return (
+        typeIsDatabaseSpecific(tableType)
+        || tableType === PrologTableTypes.Company
+        || tableType === PrologTableTypes.POCatalog
+    );
+}
+
+/**
+ * Whether the table/context allows the project-selection panel (ASP.NET still hid the div for database-specific types).
+ * Callers combine with options.PrintingVisibleItems === true to match ReportingWizard.js.
+ */
+export function shouldShowProjectSelection(
+    isForMultipleRecords: boolean,
+    tableType: number,
+    actualTableType: number
+): boolean {
+    return (
+        isForMultipleRecords &&
+        !typeMatchesReportWizardDatabaseSpecific(tableType) &&
+        (actualTableType === undefined || actualTableType === PrologTableTypes.None)
+    );
+}
+
+/**
+ * Whether the File Library section (table-type gate) is allowed. DMS access is separate (`ReportWizardConfig.showFileLibrary`).
+ * Same predicate as legacy server: `!typeMatchesReportWizardDatabaseSpecific(tableType)`.
+ */
+export function shouldShowFileLibrarySectionByTableType(tableType: number): boolean {
+    return !typeMatchesReportWizardDatabaseSpecific(tableType);
+}
+
+/**
+ * Whether the Attachments tab is allowed for this table type (same table gate as file library).
+ * Predicate: `!typeMatchesReportWizardDatabaseSpecific(tableType)`.
+ */
+export function shouldShowAttachmentsTabByTableType(tableType: number): boolean {
+    return !typeMatchesReportWizardDatabaseSpecific(tableType);
+}
+
+/**
+ * Table-level rule forcing PDF output (single-record Drawing). Client still combines with each report's `PDFOutputOnly`.
+ */
+export function forcePdfFormatByTable(tableType: number, isForMultipleRecords: boolean): boolean {
+    return tableType === PrologTableTypes.Drawing && !isForMultipleRecords;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Whether a report template belongs in the wizard list for the current Budget context.
+ * Parity: C# `Report.OkToIncludeInPrintWizardReports` (Budget table only).
+ * Callers must apply only when wizard `tableType` is Budget; otherwise include all reports.
+ */
+export function budgetReportMatchesWizardContext(
+    wizardActualTableType: number,
+    reportActualTableType: number
+): boolean {
+    switch (wizardActualTableType) {
+        case PrologTableTypes.None:
+        case PrologTableTypes.Budget:
+            switch (reportActualTableType) {
+                case PrologTableTypes.None:
+                case PrologTableTypes.Budget:
+                case PrologTableTypes.JobCostItem:
+                case PrologTableTypes.JobCosts:
+                    return true;
+                default:
+                    return false;
+            }
+        case PrologTableTypes.BudgetSnapshotItem:
+            switch (reportActualTableType) {
+                case PrologTableTypes.BudgetSnapshotItem:
+                case PrologTableTypes.JobCostItemSnapshot:
+                case PrologTableTypes.JobCostsSnapshot:
+                    return true;
+                default:
+                    return false;
+            }
+        default:
+            return false;
+    }
+}
